jsRuntime.policy: Pinned current runtime for desktop development, CI, and packaging

notes: Declared manifests were updated to current stable versions as of 2026-05-18.
The repo now uses .mise.toml as the canonical Node runtime pin and no longer carries duplicate .nvmrc or .node-version files.
The runtime contract is embedded GUI -> ffhn-core -> htmlcut-core rather than a bundled sidecar chain.
Cargo build output is intentionally redirected into managed sibling artifact roots so repository-local target trees do not accumulate.
The Tauri and Vite baselines were advanced to remove known dependency advisories rather than carrying deny or audit exceptions for fixable upstream releases.
The lint baseline now uses ESLint flat config in typed strict mode plus explicit runtime-safety rules such as eqeqeq, curly, no-var, prefer-const, and no-debugger.
The browser workbench lane instruments Vite only during Playwright runs and must capture live runtime files from the maintained GUI scenarios.

The frontend test posture is intentionally split between a Vitest unit/component lane for render-state permutations and a Playwright lane for real operator flows; the final coverage verifier enforces 100% line and branch coverage from the unit lane while still requiring Playwright instrumentation evidence.
The Vite desktop bundle target is now a desktop runtime floor (`es2020`) rather than legacy browser-brand targets, because the app ships inside Tauri WebViews instead of the public web.
